int maxLengthBetweenEqualCharacters(string s) {
    unordered_map<char, pair<int, int>> char_map;
    for (int i = 0; i < s.size(); ++i) {
        char c = s[i];
        if (char_map.find(c) == char_map.end()) {
            char_map[c] = make_pair(i, i);
        } else {
            char_map[c].second = i;
        }
    }
    int max_len = -1;
    for (const auto& entry : char_map) {
        int first = entry.second.first;
        int last = entry.second.second;
        if (first != last) {
            int current_len = last - first - 1;
            if (current_len > max_len) {
                max_len = current_len;
            }
        }
    }
    return max_len == -1 ? -1 : max_len;
}

solution:
```cpp
#include <unordered_map>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    int maxLengthBetweenEqualCharacters(string s) {
        unordered_map<char, pair<int, int>> char_map;
        for (int i = 0; i < s.size(); ++i) {
            char c = s[i];
            if (char_map.find(c) == char_map.end()) {
                char_map[c] = make_pair(i, i);
            } else {
                char_map[c].second = i;
            }
        }
        int max_len = -1;
        for (const auto& entry : char_map) {
            int first = entry.second.first;
            int last = entry.second.second;
            if (first != last) {
                int current_len = last - first - 1;
                if (current_len > max_len) {
                    max_len = current_len;
                }
            }
        }
        return max_len != -1 ? max_len : -1;
    }
};
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently tracks the first and last occurrence of each character using a hash map, then calculates the maximum substring length between these occurrences. This approach ensures linear time complexity and constant space usage, making it optimal for the given problem constraints.
